news.title: Divyansh Group acquires Ghaziabad land parcel for ₹128 crore
news.para: Divyansh Group has acquired a 12,868 sq metre land parcel in Koyal Enclave, Ghaziabad, through a Ghaziabad Development Authority auction. The company plans to develop a plotted residential project with sales potential of over ₹200 crore.

news.title: Institutional investments in real estate decline 7% YoY to $1.9 billion in Q2 2026: Report
news.para: Institutional investments in Indian real estate stood at $1.9 billion in Q2 2026, down 7% year-on-year, according to Cushman & Wakefield. Office assets accounted for 51% of total investments, while data centres contributed 40%.

news.title: NAKSHA will not affect property ownership documents, boundaries: Goa government
news.para: Existing property titles and land records remain completely secure and unaffected.  The NAKSHA pilot project is a data-mapping exercise for urban land parcels.  Rights of non-resident landowners are fully safeguarded during the survey.  Physical absence does not impact legal ownership or property rights.  This initiative aims to modernize land administration and enhance town planning.

news.title: Any deed related to properties can be registered within Goa districts
news.para: Goa citizens can now register property documents at any district sub-registrar office. This new system allows registration anywhere within the district, not just taluka-level offices. The state government will amend the Registration Act to facilitate this district-wide registration process. Online document uploads will show pendency, enabling faster appointment selection. This initiative aims to improve ease of doing business for property transactions.

news.title: Lucknow civic body to launch one-time settlement scheme for house tax defaulters
news.para: Lucknow Municipal Corporation will launch a one-time settlement scheme for tax defaulters starting August 15. This initiative offers a chance to clear pending house, water, and sewer taxes up to April 2026. Taxpayers paying the principal amount will receive waivers on accumulated interest and surcharges. Those with dues over one lakh rupees can pay in three monthly installments. The scheme aims to improve recoveries and strengthen the corporation's financial position.

news.title: Surcharge on property transactions likely as Jharkhand government eyes revenue boost for civic bodies
news.para: Jharkhand's government plans a new ten percent surcharge on property transfers. This levy aims to increase revenue for municipal bodies across the state. The proposed surcharge will apply to stamp duty and registration fees for property sales. Funds collected will support civic bodies' revenue and capital expenditures. Public feedback on the draft rules is open until July twenty-fourth.

news.title: Telangana launches Indiramma housing scheme for poor in Hyderabad Core Urban Region
news.para: The Telangana government has launched the Indiramma housing scheme for the poor. This initiative will provide one lakh houses in Hyderabad's Core Urban Region. Each flat will have a built-up area of 525 square feet. Beneficiaries will receive a Rs 5 lakh subsidy from the government. The scheme aims to construct multi-storey buildings for eligible citizens.

news.title: MahaRERA recovery warrant dashboard not updated for six months, activists question transparency
news.para: Consumer activists question MahaRERA's transparency regarding its frozen dashboard.  The online tracking system for defaulting developers has remained un-updated for six months.  Homebuyers cannot track enforcement actions and recoveries effectively.  Activists urge prompt data updates and better accountability from the regulator.  They have also sought intervention from the revenue minister for faster warrant execution.

news.title: Bombay HC nixes auction of four-acre land in housing project in Shikrapur
news.para: The Bombay High Court annulled a land auction held on April 29.  It directed the project developer to refund Rs 3.15 crore to 13 flat purchasers.  The court also ordered action against the Shirur tehsildar for auction irregularities.  The Pune district collector faced criticism for his casual court appearance.  This ruling aims to resolve a long-standing dispute for homebuyers.

news.title: Redevelopment in Ahmedabad hits cost, consent roadblock
news.para: Redevelopment projects in Ahmedabad are experiencing significant delays.  Weak housing demand and rising construction costs deter builders from new commitments.  Disagreements among society members also halt progress on many stalled projects.  Some families are now paying their own rent for vacant homes.  Developers urge complete consensus and proven builder track records for success.

news.title: ED attaches 389 properties worth ₹240 crore in Grand Venezia money laundering case
news.para: The Enforcement Directorate has provisionally attached 389 immovable properties worth ₹240.03 crore in a money laundering case linked to Bhasin Infotech and Infrastructure, Grand Venezia Commercial Towers, Satinder Singh Bhasin and others. The present market value of the attached properties is estimated to be over ₹700 crore.

news.title: Bhubaneswar  civic body to conduct survey in Aug to identify residential, commercial buildings
news.para: Bhubaneswar Municipal Corporation will conduct a survey to identify building types. This survey aims to accurately assess holding tax for revenue generation. The civic body lacks a segregated list of commercial and residential properties. New buildings will also be identified and assessed for tax purposes. This initiative follows Supreme Court observations on building conversions.

news.title: Sobha’s net profit rises 274% to ₹50.84 crore in Q1 FY27
news.para: Sobha's net profit surged 273.5 percent to ₹50.84 crore in Q1 FY27. Total income increased by 47.6 percent, reaching ₹1,330.17 crore during the quarter. Real estate sales value rose 76 percent year-on-year to ₹3,656 crore. The company sold 2.34 million sq. ft., showing a 62 percent increase. Sobha also reported negative net debt, indicating a strong cash position.

news.title: SC gives Parsvnath Developers one week to deposit dues to homebuyers, warns next step is jail
news.para: The Supreme Court has given Parsvnath Developers one final week to pay homebuyers.  Failure to deposit the full amount with 12 percent interest will result in imprisonment.  The court expressed strong disapproval of the real estate firm and its directors.  This order follows a 20-year struggle by senior citizens for their homes.  The court warned that the company's directors would face jail time if non-compliant.

news.title: FONRWA seeks action against unauthorised PGs in Noida
news.para: Noida residents' welfare association seeks action against unauthorized paying guest accommodations.  These PGs reportedly lack essential fire safety equipment and pose risks.  Residents cite parking scarcity and sanitation issues caused by these operations.  The association has repeatedly flagged concerns to the Noida Authority.  They demand a survey and closure of illegal facilities to ensure safety.

news.title: UltraTech Cement’s net profit rises 17% to ₹2,603.72 crore in Q1 FY27
news.para: UltraTech Cement’s consolidated total income increased 15.5 per cent to ₹24,778.47 crore during the quarter. Its domestic grey cement capacity crossed 200.1 million tonnes per annum.

news.title: Smartworks leases over 1,100 seats to L&T Technology Services in Pune
news.para: Smartworks has expanded its engagement with L&T Technology Services by adding over 1,100 seats in Pune. The Pune expansion is expected to generate rental revenue of about ₹55 crore over 60 months.

news.title: Goa plans to convert industrial land from lease to freehold
news.para: Goa Industrial Development Corporation is working on reforms for industrial land ownership conversion.  The corporation is also considering easing rules for plot transfers and subdivisions.  These changes align with the Centre's deregulation initiative for industrial land use.  Allottees will pay a freehold premium to extinguish reversionary rights under the new framework.  Proceeds will fund a corpus, with only interest available for operational expenditure.

news.title: Chennai civic body collects ₹103 crore property tax in 14 days
news.para: Chennai Corporation collected over ₹103 crore in property tax dues in just fourteen days. This sharp rise resulted from a special intensive collection drive held across the city. Property owners voluntarily came forward to clear their tax dues during this period. Officials are continuing the drive to recover long-pending dues from remaining owners. Payments can be made through various convenient channels for property owners.

news.title: Submit DLF City demolition and restoration records: HC to Haryana
news.para: The Punjab and Haryana High Court has ordered the state government to submit demolition and restoration details.  This action concerns properties allegedly involved in unauthorized constructions within DLF City.  The court seeks building bylaws to verify compliance with planning regulations.  It aims to protect lawful buildings while addressing violations.  The court also requires property owners to furnish relevant documentation for adjudication.
